title: Open public dataset export of normalized snapshots (with provenance)
context: The normalized snapshots produced by the ingestion pipeline are themselves a public good — a clean, provenance-tracked, normalized view of open US drinking-water compliance data. Releasing them as an open dataset with full provenance enables reuse by researchers, journalists, and other public-interest applications. The export license must be compatible with source licenses: CC0 or CC-BY-4.0 for EPA/federal public-domain sources; ODbL for any OSM-derived geodata components. No individual-level or household-level data is included.
objective: Produce and publish an open public dataset export of the normalized water-quality snapshots with full provenance: (1) snapshots include all citation metadata (publisher, dataset, URL, retrievedDate, sourceVintage, license, checksum); (2) export license compatible with source licenses (CC0 or CC-BY-4.0 for federal public-domain data; ODbL for any OSM-derived geodata); (3) no individual-level or household-level data included; (4) export is reproducible from the same source vintage + pipeline version.
acceptanceCriteria: Exported dataset includes full provenance for each record: publisher, dataset, URL, retrievedDate, sourceVintage, sourceLicense, checksum
Export license compatible with all included source licenses (CC0 or CC-BY-4.0 for EPA/federal; ODbL for OSM-derived components)
No individual-level, household-level, or controlled-access data included in the export
Export is reproducible: same source vintage + pipeline version produces the same checksummed output
Export format and provenance documented in the repo
output: Dataset: open public export of normalized water-quality snapshots with provenance in data/export/, with license and provenance documentation.
